title: Sharma, Vasquez Among Notable Voices at Amsterdam Tech Conference
body:
The TechForward Summit drew several hundred attendees to Amsterdam's Beurs van Berlage on Wednesday and Thursday, with sessions covering everything from large language models to quantum error correction.

Among the more widely cited talks was a presentation by Priya Sharma on computational approaches to protein folding that she argued could be substantially accelerated by near-term quantum hardware. Sharma, who has spoken at similar forums before, drew pointed questions from the audience about timelines and the gap between academic benchmarks and industrial application.

"The honest answer is we're closer than the skeptics think and farther than the hype suggests," she told one questioner.

Also drawing attention was Elena Vasquez, the recently hired CTO of Volta Systems, whose remarks about the company's supply chain strategy and its LuminCore deal made headlines before she had left the stage. Vasquez is new to the public-facing side of the industry — her IBM Research career was largely internal — but she handled a testy Q&A with composure.

Several speakers touched on the EU regulatory environment without mentioning specific cases. The mood, multiple attendees said, was cautiously optimistic about European competitiveness in deep tech.
